question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted topicId string (e.g., containing special characters or SQL-like payloads) via the GrpcSubscriberProperties configuration endpoint, causing expressionConverter.convert() to resolve to an attacker-controlled topic, thereby redirecting all transaction subscriptions to a tampered topic and corrupting the integrity of submitted transaction monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a topicId value matching the ExpressionConverter pattern (e.g., '${topic.malicious}') through an externally reachable configuration path, causing doConvert() to publish a new CONSENSUS_CREATE_TOPIC transaction on behalf of the monitor, effectively injecting a fraudulent topic into the transaction stream?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a gRPC response that returns a StatusRuntimeException with a non-INVALID_ARGUMENT status code, causing shouldRetry() to return true and triggering infinite retry loops (maxAttempts = Long.MAX_VALUE), exhausting monitor resources and preventing legitimate submitted transactions from being observed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a rogue gRPC endpoint return a StatusRuntimeException with Status.Code.NOT_FOUND or UNAVAILABLE to bypass the INVALID_ARGUMENT filter in shouldRetry(), causing the subscriber to retry indefinitely against a tampered endpoint and never detect that submitted transactions are being suppressed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStatusCode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-StatusRuntimeException throwable to be thrown by the gRPC layer, causing getStatusCode() to return Status.Code.UNKNOWN, which passes the shouldRetry() filter and triggers unbounded retries, masking the fact that submitted transactions are being tampered with or dropped?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cached Flux<GrpcSubscription> (Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache()) by racing to influence the topicId before the cache is populated, causing all subsequent subscribers to permanently use a tampered topicId for submitted transaction monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user set the subscribers count to the maximum (1024) via externally reachable properties, causing createSubscriptions() to spawn 1024 GrpcSubscription instances all pointing to a tampered topicId, amplifying the impact of transaction tampering across all subscription channels?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the retry backoff parameters (minBackoff, maxBackoff) to near-zero values, causing clientSubscribe() to hammer the gRPC endpoint with rapid retries after a tampered transaction causes an error, resulting in a self-inflicted denial of service that prevents legitimate transaction observation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the gRPC mirror node endpoint inject fabricated TopicMessage responses into the grpcClient.subscribe() stream, causing the monitor to record tampered transaction data as legitimate, corrupting the integrity of the submitted transaction audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a blank or null topicId that bypasses the @NotBlank validation at runtime (e.g., via property override), causing TopicId.fromString() in GrpcSubscription.getTopicMessageQuery() to throw an exception that is silently swallowed, resulting in submitted transactions never being monitored?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition in the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() initialization by sending concurrent subscription requests, causing createSubscriptions() to be called multiple times with different topicId values, resulting in inconsistent transaction monitoring where some subscribers observe tampered topics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a malicious gRPC trailer or metadata into the StatusRuntimeException returned by the mirror node, causing the log.warn() in doBeforeRetry() to log attacker-controlled content (log injection), which could be used to forge audit entries about submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a gRPC error message containing newline characters that, when passed to StringUtils.substringBefore(r.failure().getMessage(), '\\n'), causes log injection by truncating the real error and substituting attacker-controlled text, obscuring evidence of transaction tampering?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user access the getSubscriptions() endpoint to enumerate all active GrpcSubscription instances and their associated topicIds, gaining intelligence about which topics are being monitored for submitted transactions, enabling targeted tampering of only those topics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user set properties.isEnabled() to false for all legitimate subscriptions while enabling a rogue subscription pointing to an attacker-controlled topicId, causing createSubscriptions() to only create subscriptions for the tampered topic and completely bypassing legitimate submitted transaction monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the gRPC endpoint return an empty stream (Flux.empty()) from grpcClient.subscribe(), causing clientSubscribe() to complete without error and without triggering any retry, silently dropping all submitted transaction notifications while the monitor reports healthy status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: shouldRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that shouldRetry() only filters INVALID_ARGUMENT errors by causing the gRPC server to return PERMISSION_DENIED or UNAUTHENTICATED, which passes the filter and triggers retries, allowing the attacker to keep the subscriber in a perpetual retry loop while submitted transactions go unmonitored?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a topicId that resolves to a valid but attacker-controlled Hedera topic (one the attacker created and controls), causing the monitor to subscribe to attacker-published messages instead of legitimate submitted transactions, enabling full control over what the monitor observes?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer() wrapping in clientSubscribe() to cause repeated re-subscription to a tampered gRPC endpoint after each retry, where each re-subscription fetches a fresh but still-tampered TopicMessageQuery, ensuring that transaction tampering persists across all retry attempts?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user set the limit property to 0 (unlimited) and the duration to Long.MAX_VALUE nanoseconds, causing createSubscriptions() to create subscriptions that never terminate, permanently locking the monitor into observing a tampered topic with no way to reset without a full service restart?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) in subscribe() to cause unbounded concurrency by injecting a large number of GrpcSubscription objects, overwhelming the reactor scheduler and causing legitimate submitted transaction events to be delayed or dropped due to thread starvation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user who controls the gRPC mirror node selectively delay responses to submitted transaction queries (slow-drip attack), causing the retry backoff in clientSubscribe() to never trigger (since no error is thrown), while the monitor waits indefinitely for transaction confirmations that are being withheld?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: createSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a topicId containing a valid expression pattern like '${topic.x}' where 'x' maps to a scenario that creates a topic with attacker-controlled memo/content, causing ExpressionConverterImpl.doConvert() to publish a CONSENSUS_CREATE_TOPIC transaction that embeds malicious data into the Hedera ledger as part of the monitor's normal operation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: clientSubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the gRPC endpoint to return a StatusRuntimeException with Status.Code.RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, which passes shouldRetry() and triggers retries, while simultaneously the attacker floods the topic with tampered messages, causing the monitor to be overwhelmed and unable to validate the integrity of submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/grpc/GrpcSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: getStatusCode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a wrapped exception (e.g., RuntimeException wrapping a StatusRuntimeException) to be thrown, causing getStatusCode() to return UNKNOWN instead of the actual gRPC status, bypassing the INVALID_ARGUMENT check in shouldRetry() and causing the subscriber to retry on what should be a terminal error, masking transaction tampering?
